user_query:
We’re preparing for a Q2 budget review. Please run a complete AI spend audit using the provided inventory and company profile, and produce board-ready outputs. Read input/ai_inventory.csv and input/company_profile.yaml. Apply these exact rules so your calculations are reproducible:

Scoring (per tool):
- Usage score (0-30):
  - If licensed_users > 0: let r = active_users / licensed_users.
    - If active_users == 0 -> 0
    - Else if r < 0.25 -> 10
    - Else if r <= 0.75 -> 20
    - Else (> 0.75) -> 30
  - Else if utilization_pct is a number:
    - If utilization_pct == 0 -> 0
    - Else if < 25 -> 10
    - Else if 25-75 -> 20
    - Else (> 75) -> 30
  - Else -> 10
- ROI score (0-40) using measured_roi_multiplier if present:
  - If < 1.0 or blank -> 0
  - If 1.0 to < 2.0 -> 10
  - If 2.0 to 5.0 -> 30
  - If > 5.0 -> 40
- Replaceability score (0-30):
  - If category is "Foundation Models" or "SaaS with AI":
    - If capability_group has 3+ tools in the inventory -> 0
    - If capability_group has exactly 2 tools -> 10
    - If capability_group has exactly 1 tool -> 20
  - Else (Custom Development, Infrastructure, Data & Training) -> 20
- Total score = usage + roi + replaceability (0-100)
- Action by thresholds: 0-30 CUT, 31-50 REVIEW, 51-70 OPTIMIZE, 71-100 KEEP

Waste/savings calculations (monthly):
- Unused licenses (SaaS with AI or any row with licensed_users and active_users): monthly_cost * (1 - (active_users/licensed_users)) when licensed_users > 0; else 0
- Over-provisioned infra (rows with numeric utilization_pct):
  - Base: if utilization_pct < 50 then monthly_cost * (0.5 - utilization_pct/100), else 0
  - Plus: if environment == "dev" AND always_on == true then add 0.2 * monthly_cost
- Model tier downgrades (Foundation Models only): if cheaper_model_available == true then 0.3 * monthly_cost, else 0
- Vendor consolidation: group by capability_group; for any group with count > 1, savings = 0.3 * sum(monthly_cost) for that group; sum across all such groups
- Total recoverable = sum of the four components above

Benchmarks:
- Determine company_size_bracket and typical_waste_range using employee_count from input/company_profile.yaml:
  - 10-25: 35-50%, 25-50: 30-45%, 50-200: 25-40%, 200-500: 20-35%, 500+: 15-30%

Outputs to write:
1) output/report.json — JSON object with:
   - company_name, period
   - totals: { total_monthly_spend, total_annual_spend, ai_spend_percent_of_monthly_revenue }
   - waste_identified: { unused_licenses, over_provisioned_infra, model_tier_downgrades, vendor_consolidation, total_recoverable }
   - tools: array of { name, category, monthly_cost, capability_group, scores: { usage, roi, replaceability, total }, action }
   - actions: { CUT: [...names], REVIEW: [...], OPTIMIZE: [...], KEEP: [...] }
   - model_optimization: { model_selection, caching, batch_vs_realtime, token_optimization } with concrete next steps
   - vendor_consolidation: brief summary of overlapping groups and target reduction
   - benchmarks: { company_size_bracket, typical_waste_range }
   - plan_90_day: { weeks_1_2: [], weeks_3_4: [], weeks_5_8: [], weeks_9_12: [] } (actionable bullets)
2) output/scores.csv — One row per tool with columns: name,category,monthly_cost,capability_group,usage_score,roi_score,replaceability_score,total_score,action
3) output/vendor_consolidation.md — Show at least 3 explicit consolidation mappings using "->" (e.g., "Jasper + Copy.ai -> Single content tool") and a brief rationale for each
4) output/assumptions.md — Explain how you applied the scoring thresholds and each waste formula above; call out any data gaps and how you handled them

Use the 5-category inventory in the analysis (Foundation Models, SaaS with AI, Custom Development, Infrastructure, Data & Training). Compute AI spend as a percent of monthly revenue using revenue from input/company_profile.yaml. Cross-check that every row in input/ai_inventory.csv appears in both report.json (tools array) and scores.csv. Keep numbers to two decimal places where appropriate.
